title: 11 Herbal Creams For Difficulty Eating
intro_desc: Herbal creams can help with difficulty eating by soothing the digestive system. These creams are made from herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can calm down any irritation in the mouth, throat, or esophagus. For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to aid digestion. It helps to reduce nausea and pain, making it easier to eat.  Curcuma longa, or turmeric, is another herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. It's been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the digestive system, which can make eating more comfortable. Some people also use it to help with digestive issues like bloating and gas.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is another herb that can help with digestive issues. It has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe the stomach and intestines. It's also been shown to help with digestive issues like heartburn and acid reflux.  These herbal creams can be applied directly to the affected area, or taken internally in the form of a supplement. They're a natural way to relieve difficulty eating, without the harsh side effects of some medications. By using these herbal creams, people can find relief from digestive issues and be able to eat more comfortably.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort in the throat and mouth, making it easier to eat. The warming sensation caused by Zingiber officinale creams can also help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, which can be a problem when eating is difficult. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation. By addressing these underlying issues, Zingiber officinale creams can make eating more comfortable and manageable.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in the Zingiber officinale powder and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use. Apply it to the affected area as needed to help ease difficulty eating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains bioactive compounds like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties that protect the tissues from damage and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these compounds may help alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating, such as mouth pain and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (from Curcuma longa root) and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the melted mixture.
Stir the mixture well until the shea butter is fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a container for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice extract, which is rich in glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins. Glycyrrhizin is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation that may be causing difficulty eating. Flavonoids, such as luteolin and isoliquertigenin, have antioxidant properties that can protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat from damage. Saponins, like glabrene, can help reduce inflammation and improve the health of the mucous membranes, making it easier to eat. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may provide relief for people experiencing difficulty eating due to inflammation or irritation in the mouth, throat, or digestive tract.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of glycerin, 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, and 2-3 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Add the distilled water and glycerin to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains the bioactive constituents carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat. Rosmarinus officinalis creams also have antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe mouth sores and reduce the risk of infection. The antispasmodic properties of carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid can help relax the muscles in the mouth and throat, making it easier to swallow. By reducing inflammation, soothing mouth sores, and relaxing muscles, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can make eating easier for people with difficulty eating.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream for difficulty eating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and bilobalide as its active constituents. These compounds help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat. This can make eating and swallowing easier for people with difficulty eating. The anti-inflammatory properties of flavonoids also help to soothe mouth and throat irritation, reducing discomfort and pain. By improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba creams may provide relief from eating difficulties.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted and well combined.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil, stirring well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Ginkgo biloba cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and polyphenols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat. The flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea creams, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that protect the mucous membranes from damage and promote healing. The polysaccharides in Echinacea purpurea creams also stimulate the immune system and increase the production of white blood cells, which helps fight off infections that can cause difficulty eating. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams can help make eating less painful and more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a clean bowl.
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a saucepan with a thermometer to 120-140°F (49-60°C).
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the heated oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts. Add the infused oil to the saucepan and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Once cooled, use the Echinacea purpurea creams as needed to aid in difficulty eating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthol and menthone, two active constituents that help with difficulty eating. Menthol increases saliva production, which aids in the breakdown of food. Menthone has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms. The antispasmodic properties of menthone also help to relax the muscles involved in swallowing, making it easier to eat. By reducing inflammation and spasms, menthone helps to alleviate discomfort and pain associated with difficulty eating.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 1 teaspoon of Mentha x piperita essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until fully melted.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in the Mentha x piperita essential oil and aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until it reaches a creamy consistency, stirring occasionally.
Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, use a spatula to scoop it into a jar or tin, ready to use as a cream for difficulty eating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains active constituents like ginsenosides, saponins, and ginsenol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract. This can make eating and swallowing easier for people with difficulty eating. The antioxidant properties of ginsenosides also help protect the mucous membranes from damage, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Panax ginseng creams may help alleviate some of the difficulties associated with eating.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of ginseng powder (Panax ginseng), 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
In a small saucepan, heat the distilled water over low heat. Add the ginseng powder and stir until it dissolves.
In a separate bowl, mix the beeswax and coconut oil until well combined.
Slowly pour the ginseng mixture into the beeswax mixture and stir until it thickens. Add the vitamin E oil and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool before applying it to the skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains bioactive compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated tissues in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract. The gel-like substance in aloe vera creams also forms a protective barrier that helps shield the mucous membranes from further irritation, making it easier to eat and swallow. Additionally, aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, making it easier to manage difficulty eating. The soothing and protective properties of aloe vera creams can help provide relief and make eating more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
In a separate bowl, mix the aloe vera gel and shea butter until smooth.
Slowly add the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture to the aloe vera gel mixture, stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into small containers and add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to each container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols, which are absorbed quickly into the bloodstream. These active constituents help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, making it easier to eat. Silymarin also improves gut health by increasing the production of bile and enzymes that break down food. This, in turn, enhances digestion and reduces symptoms of difficulty eating, such as bloating and abdominal pain. Regular use of Silybum marianum creams may also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to swallow food.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a jar and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help with difficulty eating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a high amount of acemannan, a type of polysaccharide. Acemannan has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe mouth sores and reduce swelling. It also contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which can aid in wound healing and promote healthy tissue growth. The gel-like substance in aloe vera cream is rich in mucilages, which can help protect the mouth and esophagus from irritation and inflammation. By applying aloe barbadensis cream to the affected area, it may help make eating easier by reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and distilled water to the cooled mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your aloe vera cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with eating difficulties. Some teas, like ginger and peppermint, can ease digestion and reduce nausea. These teas can also calm the stomach, making it easier to eat and digest food. This can be especially helpful for people who have trouble eating due to stomach issues or anxiety.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option to help with difficulty eating. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and how they might interact with any medications. Some herbal teas can cause allergic reactions or worsen digestive issues. Start with small amounts and see how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: The time it takes for herbal teas to show results in difficulty eating varies. Some people notice relief from digestive issues within a few days, while others may take a week or two to feel the effects. It's also possible that results may be subtle, and you may only notice a slight improvement in your eating habits over time.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea in the morning can help with difficulty eating. It can help calm your stomach and get your digestive system moving. Many herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have natural properties that can ease nausea and discomfort, making it easier to eat a meal.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for difficulty eating. Some teas, like St. John's Wort, can change the way your body processes medications. This can affect how well they work. For example, if you take a medication to help with eating and you drink a tea that interacts with it, the medication may not work as well as it should.
faq_pregnant: When drinking herbal teas during pregnancy, it's best to be cautious about eating difficulties. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can help ease nausea and make eating easier. However, other teas, like those with caffeine, sage, and pennyroyal, might not be safe in large amounts. Always check the tea label for safety information.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for difficulty eating while breastfeeding are generally considered safe, but some may affect milk supply. Peppermint and ginger teas, for example, can help ease digestion, while chamomile tea can calm nervousness. However, teas like sage and pennyroyal may decrease milk production, so choose carefully and monitor your baby's response.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams to Combat Difficulty Eating in Seniors
